name: tutorial-script-r-clinical
title: R Script — Clinical Statistics
description: Use R for clinical data analysis within a workflow. Demonstrates R runtime in script-container, reading step input, computing summary statistics with base R, and writing structured JSON output. Essential pattern for pharma workflows where R is the validated statistical environment.

steps.name: Enter Lab Values
steps.description: Provide comma-separated lab values (e.g. ALT levels in U/L) and the Upper Limit of Normal (ULN) for the analyte.

params.name: uln
params.description: Upper Limit of Normal (ULN) for this analyte, e.g. 40

params.name: analyte
params.description: Analyte name, e.g. ALT, AST, bilirubin

steps.name: Analyze with R
steps.description: Compute clinical statistics: descriptive stats, count of values exceeding ULN thresholds (1x, 3x, 5x, 10x), and flag potential Hy's Law signals.

script.inlineScript:
library(jsonlite)

input <- fromJSON('/output/input.json')
raw_values <- input$steps$`enter-data`$values
uln <- as.numeric(input$steps$`enter-data`$uln)
analyte <- input$steps$`enter-data`$analyte

values <- as.numeric(trimws(unlist(strsplit(raw_values, ','))))
values <- values[!is.na(values)]

n <- length(values)
mean_val <- round(mean(values), 2)
median_val <- round(median(values), 2)
sd_val <- round(sd(values), 2)
min_val <- min(values)
max_val <- max(values)

xuln <- values / uln
above_1x <- sum(xuln > 1)
above_3x <- sum(xuln > 3)
above_5x <- sum(xuln > 5)
above_10x <- sum(xuln > 10)

result <- list(
  analyte = analyte,
  n = n,
  mean = mean_val,
  median = median_val,
  sd = sd_val,
  min = min_val,
  max = max_val,
  uln = uln,
  above_1xULN = above_1x,
  above_3xULN = above_3x,
  above_5xULN = above_5x,
  above_10xULN = above_10x,
  max_xULN = round(max(xuln), 2),
  summary = sprintf('%s: n=%d, mean=%.1f, max=%.1f (%.1fxULN), %d/%d above ULN',
    analyte, n, mean_val, max_val, max(xuln), above_1x, n)
)

write(toJSON(result, auto_unbox = TRUE), '/output/result.json')

steps.name: Review Analysis
steps.description: Review the computed statistics. Approve if analysis looks correct, revise to re-enter data.
